front: What is the notation P(A) called and what does it represent?
back: P(A) is the probability of event A. It represents the likelihood that event A occurs, ranging from 0 (impossible) to 1 (certain).

front: What does the notation P(A | B) represent?
back: P(A | B) is the conditional probability of event A given event B. It is the probability that A occurs, given that B is known to have occurred.

front: What is the mathematical definition of the conditional probability P(A | B)?
back: P(A | B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B), provided that P(B) > 0.

front: What does the notation P(A, B) or P(A ∩ B) represent?
back: This is the joint probability of events A and B. It represents the probability that both event A AND event B occur simultaneously.

front: What does the notation P(A ∪ B) represent and what is the formula for it?
back: P(A ∪ B) is the probability of the union of A and B. It is the probability that event A OR event B (or both) occurs. Its formula is P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B).
